assertions.title: Search Returns Ranked Results
assertions.evidence: Searched 'Flask application' in flask repo: 5 results returned with file_path, line range, code snippet, score. Exit code 0. Scores descending: True.

assertions.title: Exact Symbol Lookup
assertions.evidence: 12 symbol queries across Flask and Ripgrep repos: Flask, url_for, Blueprint, jsonify, send_file, Request, Response, make_response (flask), SearchWorker, Printer, Searcher, Regex (ripgrep). All 12 returned correct definition in top-1. 100% top-1 accuracy far exceeds 80% target.

assertions.title: Natural Language Intent Search
assertions.evidence: 6 NL intent queries on flask: 'error handling middleware' -> test_error_handling_processing, 'HTTP routing and URL mapping' -> test_werkzeug_routing/test_url_mapping, 'template rendering engine' -> render_template, 'request parsing and validation' -> relevant docs, 'session management and cookies' -> SecureCookieSessionInterface, 'configuration loading' -> Config class. All found semantically relevant code in top-5.

assertions.title: Cross-File Discovery
assertions.evidence: 3 cross-file queries: 'session interface implementation' returned results from 6 files (sessions.py, test_session_interface.py, etc.), 'Blueprint registration' from 5 files (blueprints.py, tests, docs), 'JSON response handling' from 9 files. All exceed 2+ file requirement.

assertions.title: BM25 Search Works Standalone
assertions.evidence: BM25-only mode (no embedding env vars set): 'url_for' query returned 5 results. Top-1: src/flask/helpers.py url_for function (score=12.62). All results contain keyword 'url_for'. Scores descending: True. Exact identifier matches rank highest.

assertions.title: Vector Search Works Standalone
assertions.evidence: Semantic search 'memory allocation' in ripgrep returned BufferAllocation at line_buffer.rs (rank 2) despite lexical mismatch. Top-5 all contain relevant terms (alloc, buffer, capacity, memory). Demonstrates semantic matching beyond keyword overlap.

assertions.title: Hybrid Outperforms BM25 Alone
assertions.evidence: From benchmark results: hybrid MRR@10=0.6009 vs BM25-only MRR@10=0.2820. Hybrid more than doubles BM25 performance. Per-category: intent (0.46 vs 0.07), config (0.625 vs 0.0), symbol_lookup (0.85 vs 0.75). Tested on 17 queries across 5 categories.

assertions.title: Hybrid Outperforms Vector Alone
assertions.evidence: From benchmark results: hybrid MRR@10=0.6009 vs vector-only MRR@10=0.2814 (M1 baseline). Hybrid more than doubles vector-only performance. Assertion check passed in combined_results.json.

assertions.title: Reranking Improves Precision
assertions.evidence: From benchmark results: reranked Precision@3=0.2451 vs unreranked Precision@3=0.1373 (79% improvement). Reranked Recall@10=0.7549 vs unreranked=0.6667 (improved, not degraded). Both assertion checks passed.

assertions.title: Result Structure Complete
assertions.evidence: JSON validation: all 5 results have file_path (string), line_start (int), line_end (int), content (string), language (string), score (float). Symbol matches include symbol_name and symbol_type. Scores descending: True. Snippet content verified against source file (url_for at helpers.py:200-251 matches exactly).

assertions.title: Query Latency Target
assertions.evidence: 25 warm queries on flask: p50=4081.8ms, p95=7047.5ms, p99=7906.4ms, max=7906.4ms. BM25-only: p50=2.6ms, p95=2.9ms. Cold start: 5012ms. The p95 latency far exceeds 500ms target due to remote embedding API round-trip (~4s per query). BM25 computation is <3ms. Benchmark harness confirmed: hybrid p95=6748.8ms over 34 queries. The bottleneck is entirely the remote API call for query embedding.

assertions.title: Graceful Degradation
assertions.evidence: Broken reranker (invalid URL): search returns 2 unreranked results with warning 'reranker unavailable...returning unreranked results'. Exit code 0. Broken embedding (env vars unset): falls back to BM25-only with warning 'embedding API not configured...falling back to BM25-only search'. Exit code 0. No crashes or hangs.

summary: Tested 12 assertions: 11 passed, 1 failed. VAL-RET-011 (latency) fails because remote embedding API round-trips add ~4s per query, pushing p95 to 7047ms vs 500ms target. All other retrieval assertions pass: search quality is excellent (100% top-1 symbol accuracy, semantic intent search works well, cross-file discovery returns 5-9 files), hybrid outperforms both BM25-only and vector-only on MRR@10, reranking improves precision by 79%, and graceful degradation works correctly.
